摘要：最近集成大型语言模型(LLM)的工作导致了知识库问答(KBQA)任务的显著改进。然而，我们假设现有的KBQA数据集要么具有简单的问题，要么使用综合生成的逻辑形式，要么基于小型知识库(KB)模式，并不能捕捉到KBQA任务的真正复杂性。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了菠菜数据集，这是一个专家注释的KBQA数据集，从Wikidata的“请求查询”论坛上的论坛讨论中收集，有320个去上下文的问题-SPARQL对。比现有数据集复杂得多的SPANACH需要强大的KBQA系统，这些系统不依赖于训练数据来学习知识库模式，但可以动态地探索大型且往往不完整的模式并对其进行推理。除了数据集，我们还介绍了菠菜代理，这是一种新的KBQA方法，它模仿人类专家如何为这些具有挑战性的问题编写SPARQL。在现有数据集上的实验显示了菠菜在KBQA中的能力，在F1中，在QALD-7、QALD-9 Plus和QALD-10数据集上分别达到了30.1%、27.0%和10.0%的最新水平，与WikiWebQuestions上微调的骆驼Sota模型的结果相差不到1.6%。在我们的新菠菜数据集上，菠菜代理在F1中的表现超过了所有基线，包括最好的基于GPT-4的KBQA代理，高出38.1%。科目：计算和语言(cs.CL)引用为：arxiv：2407.11417cs.CL

[NLP-38] Representation Bias in Political Sample Simulations with Large Language Models
[NLP-38] 大语言模型政治样本模拟中的表示偏差

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11409
作者: Weihong Qi,Hanjia Lyu,Jiebo Luo
关键词: Large Language Models, German Longitudinal Election, Longitudinal Election Study, National Election Studies, American National Election
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、德国纵向选举、纵向选举研究、全国选举研究、美国全国选举
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This study seeks to identify and quantify biases in simulating political samples with Large Language Models, specifically focusing on vote choice and public opinion. Using the GPT-3.5-Turbo model, we leverage data from the American National Election Studies, German Longitudinal Election Study, Zuobiao Dataset, and China Family Panel Studies to simulate voting behaviors and public opinions. This methodology enables us to examine three types of representation bias: disparities based on the the country’s language, demographic groups, and political regime types. The findings reveal that simulation performance is generally better for vote choice than for public opinions, more accurate in English-speaking countries, more effective in bipartisan systems than in multi-partisan systems, and stronger in democratic settings than in authoritarian regimes. These results contribute to enhancing our understanding and developing strategies to mitigate biases in AI applications within the field of computational social science.
摘要：本研究试图识别和量化在使用大型语言模型模拟政治样本时的偏差，特别是关注投票选择和民意。使用GPT-3.5Turbo模型，我们利用来自美国国家选举研究、德国纵向选举研究、左标数据集和中国家庭小组研究的数据来模拟投票行为和民意。这一方法使我们能够检查三种类型的代表性偏差：基于国家语言、人口群体和政治制度类型的差异。研究结果显示，投票选择的模拟表现通常比公众意见更好，在英语国家更准确，在两党制度下比在多党制度下更有效，在民主环境下比威权制度下更强。这些结果有助于提高我们的理解，并制定策略，以减轻计算社会科学领域人工智能应用中的偏差。

[NLP-39] Revisiting the Impact of Pursuing Modularity for Code Generation
[NLP-39] 重新审视追求代码生成模块化的影响

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11406
作者: Deokyeong Kang,Ki Jung Seo,Taeuk Kim
关键词: independent building blocks, integrating smaller, independent building, building blocks, software development
中文关键词: 独立的构建模块，集成较小的独立构建、构建模块、软件开发
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 9 pages, 7 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Modular programming, which aims to construct the final program by integrating smaller, independent building blocks, has been regarded as a desirable practice in software development. However, with the rise of recent code generation agents built upon large language models (LLMs), a question emerges: is this traditional practice equally effective for these new tools? In this work, we assess the impact of modularity in code generation by introducing a novel metric for its quantitative measurement. Surprisingly, unlike conventional wisdom on the topic, we find that modularity is not a core factor for improving the performance of code generation models. We also explore potential explanations for why LLMs do not exhibit a preference for modular code compared to non-modular code.
摘要：模块化编程旨在通过集成较小的独立构建块来构建最终程序，被认为是软件开发中理想的实践。然而，随着最近基于大型语言模型（LLM）的代码生成代理的兴起，一个问题出现了：这种传统实践对于这些新工具是否同样有效？在这项工作中，我们通过引入一种新颖的定量测量指标来评估模块化在代码生成中的影响。令人惊讶的是，与该主题的传统观点不同，我们发现模块化并不是提高代码生成模型性能的核心因素。我们还探讨了为什么与非模块化代码相比，LLM不表现出对模块化代码的偏好的潜在解释。

[NLP-40] CIC-BART-SSA: Controllable Image Captioning with Structured Semantic Augmentation
[NLP-40] CIC-BART-ZA：具有结构化语义增强的可控图像字幕

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11393
作者: Kalliopi Basioti,Mohamed A. Abdelsalam,Federico Fancellu,Vladimir Pavlovic,Afsaneh Fazly
关键词: Controllable Image Captioning, generating natural language, natural language descriptions, Controllable Image, aims at generating
中文关键词: 可控图像字幕，生成自然语言，自然语言描述，可控图像，旨在生成
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Accepted to ECCV 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Controllable Image Captioning (CIC) aims at generating natural language descriptions for an image, conditioned on information provided by end users, e.g., regions, entities or events of interest. However, available image–language datasets mainly contain captions that describe the entirety of an image, making them ineffective for training CIC models that can potentially attend to any subset of regions or relationships. To tackle this challenge, we propose a novel, fully automatic method to sample additional focused and visually grounded captions using a unified structured semantic representation built on top of the existing set of captions associated with an image. We leverage Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR), a cross-lingual graph-based semantic formalism, to encode all possible spatio-semantic relations between entities, beyond the typical spatial-relations-only focus of current methods. We use this Structured Semantic Augmentation (SSA) framework to augment existing image–caption datasets with the grounded controlled captions, increasing their spatial and semantic diversity and focal coverage. We then develop a new model, CIC-BART-SSA, specifically tailored for the CIC task, that sources its control signals from SSA-diversified datasets. We empirically show that, compared to SOTA CIC models, CIC-BART-SSA generates captions that are superior in diversity and text quality, are competitive in controllability, and, importantly, minimize the gap between broad and highly focused controlled captioning performance by efficiently generalizing to the challenging highly focused scenarios. Code is available at this https URL.
摘要：可控图像字幕(CIC)的目标是根据最终用户提供的信息，例如感兴趣的区域、实体或事件，为图像生成自然语言描述。然而，可用的图像语言数据集主要包含描述整个图像的字幕，这使得它们对于训练可能关注任何区域或关系子集的CIC模型无效。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的、全自动的方法，使用建立在与图像关联的现有字幕集之上的统一结构化语义表示来对额外的聚焦和视觉基础字幕进行采样。我们利用抽象意义表示(AMR)，这是一种基于图形的跨语言语义形式化方法，来编码实体之间所有可能的空间-语义关系，而不是当前方法仅关注空间关系的典型方法。我们使用这种结构化的语义增强(SSA)框架来用扎根的受控字幕来扩充现有的图像字幕数据集，增加了它们的空间和语义多样性和焦点覆盖。然后，我们开发了一个新的模型，CIC-BART-SSA，专门为CIC任务量身定做，它的控制信号来自SSA多样化的数据集。我们的实验表明，与SOTA CIC模型相比，CIC-BART-SSA生成的字幕在多样性和文本质量方面具有优势，在可控性方面具有竞争力，重要的是，通过有效地泛化到具有挑战性的高度聚焦的场景，缩小了宽泛字幕和高度聚焦字幕之间的差距。代码可在此HTTPS URL上找到。

[NLP-41] InvAgent: A Large Language Model based Multi-Agent System for Inventory Management in Supply Chains
[NLP-41] InvAgent：一个基于大语言模型的供应链库存管理多Agent系统

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11384
作者: Yinzhu Quan,Zefang Liu
关键词: deliver products efficiently, involves coordinating, flow of goods, products efficiently, coordinating the flow
中文关键词: 高效交付产品，涉及协调，货物流动，产品高效，协调流程
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Supply chain management (SCM) involves coordinating the flow of goods, information, and finances across various entities to deliver products efficiently. Effective inventory management is crucial in today’s volatile, uncertain, complex, and ambiguous (VUCA) world. Previous research has demonstrated the superiority of heuristic methods and reinforcement learning applications in inventory management. However, the application of large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents in multi-agent systems for inventory management remains underexplored. This study introduces a novel approach using LLMs to manage multi-agent inventory systems. Leveraging their zero-shot learning capabilities, our model, InvAgent, enhances resilience and improves efficiency across the supply chain network. Our contributions include utilizing LLMs for zero-shot learning to enable adaptive and informed decision-making without prior training, providing significant explainability and clarity through Chain-of-Thought (CoT), and demonstrating dynamic adaptability to varying demand scenarios while minimizing costs and avoiding stockouts. Extensive evaluations across different scenarios highlight the efficiency of our model in SCM.
摘要：供应链管理(SCM)涉及协调不同实体之间的货物、信息流和资金流，以高效地交付产品。在当今动荡、不确定、复杂和不明确(VUCA)的世界里，有效的库存管理至关重要。以往的研究已经证明了启发式方法和强化学习在库存管理中的应用的优越性。然而，在库存管理的多智能体系统中，大语言模型(LLM)作为自治智能体的应用还不够深入。提出了一种利用LLMS管理多智能体库存系统的新方法。利用他们的零概率学习能力，我们的模型InvAgent增强了整个供应链网络的弹性并提高了效率。我们的贡献包括利用LLMS进行零距离学习，在无需事先培训的情况下进行适应性和知情决策；通过思想链(COT)提供显著的可解释性和清晰性；展示对不同需求场景的动态适应性，同时最大限度地降低成本和避免缺货。跨不同场景的广泛评估突出了我们的模型在供应链管理中的效率。

[NLP-42] Reliable Reasoning Beyond Natural Language
[NLP-42] 超越自然语言的可靠推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11373
作者: Nasim Borazjanizadeh,Steven T. Piantadosi
关键词: Large Language models, Large Language, linguistic competence, reliably and flexibly, exhibit limitations
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、大型语言、语言能力、可靠且灵活，表现出局限性
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Despite their linguistic competence, Large Language models (LLMs) often exhibit limitations in their ability to reason reliably and flexibly. To address this, we propose a neurosymbolic approach that prompts LLMs to extract and encode all relevant information from a problem statement as logical code statements, and then use a logic programming language (Prolog) to conduct the iterative computations of explicit deductive reasoning. Our approach significantly enhances the performance of LLMs on the standard mathematical reasoning benchmark, GSM8k, and the Navigate dataset from the BIG-bench dataset. Additionally, we introduce a novel dataset, the Non-Linear Reasoning (NLR) dataset, consisting of 55 unique word problems that target the shortcomings of the next token prediction paradigm of LLMs and require complex non-linear reasoning but only basic arithmetic skills to solve. Our findings demonstrate that the integration of Prolog enables LLMs to achieve high performance on the NLR dataset, which even the most advanced language models (including GPT4) fail to solve using text only.
摘要：尽管大型语言模型具有语言能力，但它们在可靠和灵活的推理能力方面往往表现出局限性。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一种神经符号方法，该方法促使LLM从问题陈述中提取所有相关信息并将其编码为逻辑代码语句，然后使用逻辑编程语言(PROLOG)进行显式演绎推理的迭代计算。我们的方法显著提高了LLMS在标准数学推理基准GSM8k和来自大长凳数据集的导航数据集上的性能。此外，我们引入了一个新的数据集，非线性推理(NLR)数据集，由55个独特的应用题组成，这些问题针对LLMS的下一个令牌预测范例的缺点，需要复杂的非线性推理，但只需要基本的算术技能来解决。我们的发现表明，PROLOG的集成使LLMS能够在NLR数据集上获得高性能，而即使是最高级的语言模型(包括GPT4)也无法仅使用文本来解决这一问题。

[NLP-43] Estimating Agreement by Chance for Sequence Annotation
[NLP-43] 通过序列注释的机会估计一致性

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11371
作者: Diya Li,Carolyn Rosé,Ao Yuan,Chunxiao Zhou
关键词: natural language processing, sequence annotation tasks, chance agreement plays, language processing, natural language
中文关键词: 自然语言处理、序列注释任务、机会协议游戏、语言处理、自然语言
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: ACL 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In the field of natural language processing, correction of performance assessment for chance agreement plays a crucial role in evaluating the reliability of annotations. However, there is a notable dearth of research focusing on chance correction for assessing the reliability of sequence annotation tasks, despite their widespread prevalence in the field. To address this gap, this paper introduces a novel model for generating random annotations, which serves as the foundation for estimating chance agreement in sequence annotation tasks. Utilizing the proposed randomization model and a related comparison approach, we successfully derive the analytical form of the distribution, enabling the computation of the probable location of each annotated text segment and subsequent chance agreement estimation. Through a combination simulation and corpus-based evaluation, we successfully assess its applicability and validate its accuracy and efficacy.
摘要：在自然语言处理领域，机会一致的性能评估的纠正对于评估注释的可靠性起着至关重要的作用。然而，尽管序列注释任务在该领域广泛流行，但重点关注机会修正以评估序列注释任务可靠性的研究却显着缺乏。为了解决这一差距，本文引入了一种生成随机注释的新型模型，该模型作为估计序列注释任务中机会一致性的基础。利用提出的随机化模型和相关的比较方法，我们成功地推导出分布的分析形式，从而能够计算每个注释文本片段的可能位置以及随后的机会一致性估计。通过模拟和基于数据库的评估相结合，我们成功评估了其适用性并验证了其准确性和有效性。

[NLP-44] A Pilot Study of GSLM-based Simulation of Foreign Accentuation Only Using Native Speech Corpora
[NLP-44] 基于GLM仅使用母语语音库模拟外国口音的初步研究

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11370
作者: Kentaro Onda,Joonyong Park,Nobuaki Minematsu,Daisuke Saito
关键词: Spoken Language Model, Generative Spoken Language, native speech corpora, Language Model, Generative Spoken
中文关键词: 口语模型，生成口语，母语言语，语言模型，生成口语
类目: ound (cs.SD); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: Accepted to INTERSPEECH2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We propose a method of simulating the human process of foreign accentuation using Generative Spoken Language Model (GSLM) only with native speech corpora. When one listens to spoken words of a foreign language and repeats them, the repeated speech is often with the accent of that listener’s L1. This is said to be because the spoken words are mentally represented as a sequence of phonological units of the L1, and those units are used for oral reproduction. We simulate this process by inputting speech of language A into GSLM of language B to add B’s accent onto the input speech. The process of running ASR of the L1 for foreign input speech and giving the ASR result to TTS of the L1 can be viewed as a naive implementation of this approach. The results of our experiments show that the synthesized accent of the output speech is highly natural, compared to real samples of A generated by speakers whose L1 is B, and that the degree of accentuation is controllable.
摘要：我们提出了一种仅使用母语语音库使用生成口语模型（GLAM）来模拟人类外语重读过程的方法。当一个人听外语口语并重复它们时，重复的言语通常具有该听众L1的口音。据说这是因为口语在精神上被表示为L1语言的一系列音素单位，这些单位用于口头复制。我们通过将A语言的语音输入到B语言的GLAM中来模拟这个过程，以将B的口音添加到输入语音中。对外来输入语音运行L1的ASB并将ASB结果提供给L1的TTC的过程可以被视为该方法的天真实现。我们的实验结果表明，与L1为B的说话者生成的A的真实样本相比，输出语音的合成口音高度自然，并且加重程度是可控的。

[NLP-45] Ancient Korean Archive Translation: Comparison Analysis on Statistical phrase alignment LLM in-context learning and inter-methodological approach
[NLP-45] 古代韩国档案翻译：统计短语排列的比较分析LLM上下文学习和跨方法论方法

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11368
作者: Sojung Lucia Kim,Taehong Jang,Joonmo Ahn
关键词: in-context LLM learning, machine translation method, statistical translation method, traditional statistical translation, statistical machine translation
中文关键词: 上下文LLM学习、机器翻译方法、统计翻译方法、传统统计翻译、统计机器翻译
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: ACL2024 submitted

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This study aims to compare three methods for translating ancient texts with sparse corpora: (1) the traditional statistical translation method of phrase alignment, (2) in-context LLM learning, and (3) proposed inter methodological approach - statistical machine translation method using sentence piece tokens derived from unified set of source-target corpus. The performance of the proposed approach in this study is 36.71 in BLEU score, surpassing the scores of SOLAR-10.7B context learning and the best existing Seq2Seq model. Further analysis and discussion are presented.
摘要：本研究旨在比较用稀疏数据库翻译古代文本的三种方法：（1）传统的短语对齐统计翻译方法，（2）上下文LLM学习，（3）提出的交互方法方法–使用来自统一源目标数据库的句子片段标记的统计机器翻译方法。本研究中提出的方法的BLEU评分为36.71，超过了SOlar-10.7B上下文学习的评分和现有的最佳Seq 2Seq模型。并进行了进一步的分析和讨论。

[NLP-46] Beyond Binary: Multiclass Paraphasia Detection with Generative Pretrained Transformers and End-to-End Models
[NLP-46] 超越二进制：使用生成性预训练变形器和端到端模型进行多类失语症检测

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11345
作者: Matthew Perez,Aneesha Sampath,Minxue Niu,Emily Mower Provost
关键词: invention of words, language disorder, paraphasia detection, Automatic paraphasia detection, paraphasia
中文关键词: 发明文字、语言障碍、异语检测、自动异语检测、异语
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Aphasia is a language disorder that can lead to speech errors known as paraphasias, which involve the misuse, substitution, or invention of words. Automatic paraphasia detection can help those with Aphasia by facilitating clinical assessment and treatment planning options. However, most automatic paraphasia detection works have focused solely on binary detection, which involves recognizing only the presence or absence of a paraphasia. Multiclass paraphasia detection represents an unexplored area of research that focuses on identifying multiple types of paraphasias and where they occur in a given speech segment. We present novel approaches that use a generative pretrained transformer (GPT) to identify paraphasias from transcripts as well as two end-to-end approaches that focus on modeling both automatic speech recognition (ASR) and paraphasia classification as multiple sequences vs. a single sequence. We demonstrate that a single sequence model outperforms GPT baselines for multiclass paraphasia detection.
摘要：失语症是一种语言障碍，可导致言语错误，称为失语症，涉及词的误用、替代或发明。自动失语症检测可以通过促进临床评估和治疗计划选择来帮助失语症患者。然而，大多数自动失语症检测工作仅专注于二进制检测，这涉及到仅识别失语症的存在或不存在。多类失语症检测代表了一个未被探索的研究领域，其重点是识别多种类型的失语症以及它们在给定的语音片段中出现的位置。我们提出了使用生成性预训练转换器(GPT)从转录中识别失语症的新方法，以及两种端到端方法，其重点是将自动语音识别(ASR)和失语症分类建模为多个序列与单个序列。我们证明，在多类失语检测中，单序列模型的性能优于GPT基线。

[NLP-47] Uncertainty is Fragile: Manipulating Uncertainty in Large Language Models
[NLP-47] 不确定性是脆弱的：在大型语言模型中操纵不确定性

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11282
作者: Qingcheng Zeng,Mingyu Jin,Qinkai Yu,Zhenting Wang,Wenyue Hua,Zihao Zhou,Guangyan Sun,Yanda Meng,Shiqing Ma,Qifan Wang,Felix Juefei-Xu,Kaize Ding,Fan Yang,Ruixiang Tang,Yongfeng Zhang
关键词: Large Language Models, Large Language, Language Models, uncertainty estimation, Large
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，大型语言，语言模型，不确定性估计，大型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) are employed across various high-stakes domains, where the reliability of their outputs is crucial. One commonly used method to assess the reliability of LLMs’ responses is uncertainty estimation, which gauges the likelihood of their answers being correct. While many studies focus on improving the accuracy of uncertainty estimations for LLMs, our research investigates the fragility of uncertainty estimation and explores potential attacks. We demonstrate that an attacker can embed a backdoor in LLMs, which, when activated by a specific trigger in the input, manipulates the model’s uncertainty without affecting the final output. Specifically, the proposed backdoor attack method can alter an LLM’s output probability distribution, causing the probability distribution to converge towards an attacker-predefined distribution while ensuring that the top-1 prediction remains unchanged. Our experimental results demonstrate that this attack effectively undermines the model’s self-evaluation reliability in multiple-choice questions. For instance, we achieved a 100 attack success rate (ASR) across three different triggering strategies in four models. Further, we investigate whether this manipulation generalizes across different prompts and domains. This work highlights a significant threat to the reliability of LLMs and underscores the need for future defenses against such attacks. The code is available at this https URL.
摘要：大型语言模型(LLM)被广泛应用于各种高风险领域，其中其输出的可靠性至关重要。评估LLMS回答可靠性的一种常用方法是不确定性估计，它衡量他们回答正确的可能性。虽然许多研究都集中在提高LLMS不确定性估计的准确性上，但我们的研究调查了不确定性估计的脆弱性，并探索了潜在的攻击。我们演示了攻击者可以在LLMS中嵌入后门，当它被输入中的特定触发器激活时，在不影响最终输出的情况下操纵模型的不确定性。具体地说，提出的后门攻击方法可以改变LLM的输出概率分布，使概率分布收敛到攻击者预定义的分布，同时确保TOP-1预测保持不变。我们的实验结果表明，这种攻击有效地破坏了该模型在选择题中的自我评价可靠性。例如，我们在四个模型中的三种不同触发策略中实现了100%的攻击成功率(ASR)。此外，我们还研究了这种操作是否适用于不同的提示和域。这项工作突出了对LLMS可靠性的重大威胁，并强调了今后对这类攻击采取防御措施的必要性。代码可在此HTTPS URL上找到。

[NLP-48] arget conversation extraction: Source separation using turn-taking dynamics
[NLP-48] 目标对话提取：使用轮流动力学进行源分离

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11277
作者: Tuochao Chen,Qirui Wang,Bohan Wu,Malek Itani,Emre Sefik Eskimez,Takuya Yoshioka,Shyamnath Gollakota
关键词: Extracting the speech, amidst interfering speakers, challenging problem, presents a challenging, conversation amidst interfering
中文关键词: 在干扰的说话者中提取语音，这是一个具有挑战性的问题，在干扰中呈现了一个具有挑战性的对话
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: Accepted by Interspeech 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Extracting the speech of participants in a conversation amidst interfering speakers and noise presents a challenging problem. In this paper, we introduce the novel task of target conversation extraction, where the goal is to extract the audio of a target conversation based on the speaker embedding of one of its participants. To accomplish this, we propose leveraging temporal patterns inherent in human conversations, particularly turn-taking dynamics, which uniquely characterize speakers engaged in conversation and distinguish them from interfering speakers and noise. Using neural networks, we show the feasibility of our approach on English and Mandarin conversation datasets. In the presence of interfering speakers, our results show an 8.19 dB improvement in signal-to-noise ratio for 2-speaker conversations and a 7.92 dB improvement for 2-4-speaker conversations. Code, dataset available at this https URL.
摘要：在干扰的说话者和噪音中提取对话参与者的语音是一个具有挑战性的问题。本文介绍了目标对话提取的新颖任务，其目标是根据其中一个参与者的说话者嵌入来提取目标对话的音频。为了实现这一目标，我们建议利用人类对话中固有的时间模式，特别是轮流动态，它独特地描述了参与对话的说话者，并将他们与干扰说话者和噪音区分开来。使用神经网络，我们展示了我们方法在英语和普通话对话数据集上的可行性。在存在干扰说话者的情况下，我们的结果显示，2- 4说话者对话的信噪比提高了8.19分贝，2-4说话者对话的信噪比提高了7.92分贝。代码、数据集可在此httpsURL中找到。

[NLP-49] Making New Connections: LLMs as Puzzle Generators for The New York Times Connections Word Game
[NLP-49] 建立新的联系：法学硕士作为《纽约时报》联系文字游戏的益智生成者

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11240
作者: Tim Merino,Sam Earle,Ryan Sudhakaran,Shyam Sudhakaran,Julian Togelius
关键词: York Times, Connections, NYT, Connections puzzles, Large Language Models
中文关键词: 约克时报、Connections、NYT、Connections谜题、大型语言模型
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The Connections puzzle is a word association game published daily by The New York Times (NYT). In this game, players are asked to find groups of four words that are connected by a common theme. While solving a given Connections puzzle requires both semantic knowledge and abstract reasoning, generating novel puzzles additionally requires a form of metacognition: generators must be able to accurately model the downstream reasoning of potential solvers. In this paper, we investigate the ability of the GPT family of Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate challenging and creative word games for human players. We start with an analysis of the word game Connections and the unique challenges it poses as a Procedural Content Generation (PCG) domain. We then propose a method for generating Connections puzzles using LLMs by adapting a Tree of Thoughts (ToT) prompting approach. We evaluate this method by conducting a user study, asking human players to compare AI-generated puzzles against published Connections puzzles. Our findings show that LLMs are capable puzzle creators, and can generate diverse sets of enjoyable, challenging, and creative Connections puzzles as judged by human users.
摘要：《联系谜题》是《纽约时报》每日出版的一款词汇联想游戏。在这个游戏中，玩家被要求找出由一个共同的主题连接起来的四个单词的组。虽然解决给定的连接谜题需要语义知识和抽象推理，但生成新的谜题还需要一种形式的元认知：生成器必须能够准确地模拟潜在解算者的下游推理。在本文中，我们调查了GPT家族的大型语言模型(LLM)为人类玩家生成具有挑战性和创造性的文字游戏的能力。我们首先分析文字游戏的联系，以及它作为过程性内容生成(PCG)领域带来的独特挑战。然后，我们提出了一种采用思想树(TOT)提示方法，使用LLMS生成连接谜题的方法。我们通过进行一项用户研究来评估这种方法，要求人类玩家将人工智能生成的谜题与发布的连接谜题进行比较。我们的发现表明，LLMS是有能力的谜题创建者，并且可以根据人类用户的判断生成各种有趣的、具有挑战性的和创造性的连接谜题。

[NLP-50] Unraveling the Truth: Do LLMs really Understand Charts? A Deep Dive into Consistency and Robustness
[NLP-50] 揭开真相：法学硕士真的理解图表吗？深入探讨一致性和稳健性

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11229
作者: Srija Mukhopadhyay,Adnan Qidwai,Aparna Garimella,Pritika Ramu,Vivek Gupta,Dan Roth
关键词: Visual Language Understanding, Language Understanding, Visual Language Models, current Visual Language, Visual Language
中文关键词: 视觉语言理解，语言理解，视觉语言模型，当前视觉语言，视觉语言
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 22 pages, 7 Tables, 3 Figures, 25 examples

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Chart question answering (CQA) is a crucial area of Visual Language Understanding. However, the robustness and consistency of current Visual Language Models (VLMs) in this field remain under-explored. This paper evaluates state-of-the-art VLMs on comprehensive datasets, developed specifically for this study, encompassing diverse question categories and chart formats. We investigate two key aspects: 1) the models’ ability to handle varying levels of chart and question complexity, and 2) their robustness across different visual representations of the same underlying data. Our analysis reveals significant performance variations based on question and chart types, highlighting both strengths and weaknesses of current models. Additionally, we identify areas for improvement and propose future research directions to build more robust and reliable CQA systems. This study sheds light on the limitations of current models and paves the way for future advancements in the field.
摘要：图表问答（CQA）是视觉语言理解的一个重要领域。然而，该领域当前视觉语言模型（VLM）的稳健性和一致性仍然没有得到充分的探索。本文评估了专门为本研究开发的全面数据集上的最新VLM，涵盖了不同的问题类别和图表格式。我们研究了两个关键方面：1）模型处理不同级别图表和问题复杂性的能力，2）它们在相同基础数据的不同视觉表示中的稳健性。我们的分析揭示了基于问题和图表类型的显着性能差异，突出了当前模型的优点和缺点。此外，我们还确定了需要改进的领域，并提出未来的研究方向，以构建更强大、更可靠的CQA系统。这项研究揭示了当前模型的局限性，并为该领域的未来进步铺平了道路。

[NLP-51] Mechanistic interpretability of large language models with applications to the financial services industry
[NLP-51] 大型语言模型的机械解释性及其在金融服务行业的应用

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11215
作者: Ashkan Golgoon,Khashayar Filom,Arjun Ravi Kannan
关键词: Generative Pre-trained Transformers, Generative Pre-trained, exhibit remarkable capabilities, Large Language Models, Pre-trained Transformers
中文关键词: 生成性预训练变形机，生成性预训练，表现出非凡的能力，大型语言模型，预训练变形机
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computational Engineering, Finance, and Science (cs.CE); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Numerical Analysis (math.NA)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models such as GPTs (Generative Pre-trained Transformers) exhibit remarkable capabilities across a broad spectrum of applications. Nevertheless, due to their intrinsic complexity, these models present substantial challenges in interpreting their internal decision-making processes. This lack of transparency poses critical challenges when it comes to their adaptation by financial institutions, where concerns and accountability regarding bias, fairness, and reliability are of paramount importance. Mechanistic interpretability aims at reverse engineering complex AI models such as transformers. In this paper, we are pioneering the use of mechanistic interpretability to shed some light on the inner workings of large language models for use in financial services applications. We offer several examples of how algorithmic tasks can be designed for compliance monitoring purposes. In particular, we investigate GPT-2 Small’s attention pattern when prompted to identify potential violation of Fair Lending laws. Using direct logit attribution, we study the contributions of each layer and its corresponding attention heads to the logit difference in the residual stream. Finally, we design clean and corrupted prompts and use activation patching as a causal intervention method to localize our task completion components further. We observe that the (positive) heads 10.2 (head 2 , layer 10 ), 10.7 , and 11.3 , as well as the (negative) heads 9.6 and 10.6 play a significant role in the task completion.
摘要：大型语言模型，如GPTS(生成性预先训练的转换器)，在广泛的应用程序中显示出非凡的能力。然而，由于其内在的复杂性，这些模型在解释其内部决策过程方面提出了巨大的挑战。这种缺乏透明度对金融机构的适应构成了严峻的挑战，在这些机构中，对偏见、公平性和可靠性的担忧和问责至关重要。机械可解释性旨在对复杂的人工智能模型(如变压器)进行逆向工程。在这篇文章中，我们率先使用机械可解释性来阐明用于金融服务应用程序的大型语言模型的内部工作原理。我们提供了几个示例，说明如何将算法任务设计用于遵从性监控目的。特别是，我们调查了GPT-2 Small在被提示识别潜在违反公平贷款法的情况下的注意模式。利用直接Logit归因，研究了各层及其对应的关注头对残差流中Logit差异的贡献。最后，我们设计了干净和损坏的提示，并使用激活补丁作为因果干预方法来进一步本地化我们的任务完成组件。我们观察到，(正)头10.2(头2，层10)、10.7和11.3以及(负)头9.6和10.6在任务完成中起着显著的作用。

[NLP-52] PutnamBench: Evaluating Neural Theorem-Provers on the Putnam Mathematical Competition
[NLP-52] 普特南长凳：评估普特南数学竞赛的神经理论证明者

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11214
作者: George Tsoukalas,Jasper Lee,John Jennings,Jimmy Xin,Michelle Ding,Michael Jennings,Amitayush Thakur,Swarat Chaudhuri
关键词: William Lowell Putnam, Lowell Putnam Mathematical, Putnam Mathematical Competition, North America, competition mathematics problems
中文关键词: 威廉·洛厄尔·普特南（William Lowell Putnam），洛厄尔·普特南数学，普特南数学竞赛，北美，竞赛数学问题
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Logic in Computer Science (cs.LO); Programming Languages (cs.PL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present PutnamBench, a new multilingual benchmark for evaluating the ability of neural theorem-provers to solve competition mathematics problems. PutnamBench consists of 1697 hand-constructed formalizations of 640 theorems sourced from the William Lowell Putnam Mathematical Competition, the premier undergraduate-level mathematics competition in North America. All the theorems have formalizations in Lean 4 and Isabelle; a substantial subset also has Coq formalizations. Proving the theorems requires significant problem-solving ability and proficiency in a broad range of topics taught in undergraduate mathematics courses. We use PutnamBench to evaluate several established neural and symbolic theorem-provers. These approaches can only solve a handful of the PutnamBench problems, establishing the benchmark as a difficult open challenge for research on neural theorem-proving. PutnamBench is available at this https URL.
摘要：我们介绍了PutnamBench，这是一个新的多语言基准，用于评估神经理论证明者解决竞赛数学问题的能力。PutnamBench由640个定理的1697个手工构建的形式化组成，该定理来自北美首屈一指的本科数学竞赛威廉·洛厄尔·普特南数学竞赛。所有定理都在Lean 4和Isabelle中进行了形式化;一个相当大的子集也具有Coq形式化。证明这些定理需要强大的解决问题能力和对本科数学课程中教授的广泛主题的熟练程度。我们使用PutnamBench来评估几个已建立的神经和符号理论证明者。这些方法只能解决少数PutnamBench问题，从而将基准确立为神经理论证明研究的一个困难的开放挑战。PutnamBench可通过此https URL获取。

[NLP-53] Automated essay scoring in Arabic: a dataset and analysis of a BERT-based system
[NLP-53] 阿拉伯语自动论文评分：基于BERT的系统的数据集和分析

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11212
作者: Rayed Ghazawi,Edwin Simpson
关键词: holds significant promise, provide timely feedback, Automated Essay Scoring, mark larger volumes, Automated Essay
中文关键词: 拥有重大承诺，提供及时反馈，自动论文评分，标记更大卷，自动论文
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Automated Essay Scoring (AES) holds significant promise in the field of education, helping educators to mark larger volumes of essays and provide timely feedback. However, Arabic AES research has been limited by the lack of publicly available essay data. This study introduces AR-AES, an Arabic AES benchmark dataset comprising 2046 undergraduate essays, including gender information, scores, and transparent rubric-based evaluation guidelines, providing comprehensive insights into the scoring process. These essays come from four diverse courses, covering both traditional and online exams. Additionally, we pioneer the use of AraBERT for AES, exploring its performance on different question types. We find encouraging results, particularly for Environmental Chemistry and source-dependent essay questions. For the first time, we examine the scale of errors made by a BERT-based AES system, observing that 96.15 percent of the errors are within one point of the first human marker’s prediction, on a scale of one to five, with 79.49 percent of predictions matching exactly. In contrast, additional human markers did not exceed 30 percent exact matches with the first marker, with 62.9 percent within one mark. These findings highlight the subjectivity inherent in essay grading, and underscore the potential for current AES technology to assist human markers to grade consistently across large classes.
摘要：自动作文评分(AES)在教育领域有着重要的应用前景，它可以帮助教育工作者批改大量的作文并提供及时的反馈。然而，由于缺乏公开可用的论文数据，阿拉伯语的AES研究一直受到限制。这项研究介绍了AR-AES，一个阿拉伯的AES基准数据集，包括2046篇本科生论文，包括性别信息、分数和基于标准的透明评估指南，为评分过程提供了全面的见解。这些作文来自四门不同的课程，既包括传统考试，也包括在线考试。此外，我们率先在AES中使用了AraBERT，探索了它在不同问题类型上的性能。我们发现了令人鼓舞的结果，特别是在环境化学和来源相关的论文问题上。我们首次检查了基于BERT的AES系统所造成的错误规模，观察到96.15%的错误在第一个人类标记的预测范围内，范围为1到5，79.49%的预测完全匹配。相比之下，其他人类标记与第一个标记的精确匹配率不超过30%，一个标记内有62.9%。这些发现突出了作文评分固有的主观性，并强调了当前的AES技术在帮助人类评分员在大班中保持一致评分的潜力。

[NLP-54] Unconstrained Open Vocabulary Image Classification: Zero-Shot Transfer from Text to Image via CLIP Inversion
[NLP-54] 无约束开放词汇图像分类：通过CLIP倒置从文本到图像的零镜头传输

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11211
作者: Philipp Allgeuer,Kyra Ahrens,Stefan Wermter
关键词: Vocabulary Image Classifier, innovative uNconstrained Open, uNconstrained Open Vocabulary, Open Vocabulary Image, Open Vocabulary
中文关键词: 词汇图像分类器、创新的uN约束开放、uN约束开放词汇、开放词汇图像、开放词汇词汇
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We introduce NOVIC, an innovative uNconstrained Open Vocabulary Image Classifier that uses an autoregressive transformer to generatively output classification labels as language. Leveraging the extensive knowledge of CLIP models, NOVIC harnesses the embedding space to enable zero-shot transfer from pure text to images. Traditional CLIP models, despite their ability for open vocabulary classification, require an exhaustive prompt of potential class labels, restricting their application to images of known content or context. To address this, we propose an “object decoder” model that is trained on a large-scale 92M-target dataset of templated object noun sets and LLM-generated captions to always output the object noun in question. This effectively inverts the CLIP text encoder and allows textual object labels to be generated directly from image-derived embedding vectors, without requiring any a priori knowledge of the potential content of an image. The trained decoders are tested on a mix of manually and web-curated datasets, as well as standard image classification benchmarks, and achieve fine-grained prompt-free prediction scores of up to 87.5%, a strong result considering the model must work for any conceivable image and without any contextual clues.
摘要：我们介绍了Novic，一个创新的无约束开放词汇图像分类器，它使用一个自回归转换器来生成性地输出分类标签作为语言。利用剪辑模型的广泛知识，Novic利用嵌入空间实现了从纯文本到图像的零镜头传输。传统的剪辑模型尽管具有开放词汇分类的能力，但需要详尽地提示潜在的类别标签，从而将其应用于已知内容或上下文的图像。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一个“对象解码器”模型，该模型在一个大规模的92M目标数据集上进行训练，该数据集包括模板化的对象名词集和LLM生成的字幕，以始终输出有问题的对象名词。这有效地颠倒了剪辑文本编码器，并允许从图像导出的嵌入向量直接生成文本对象标签，而不需要图像的潜在内容的任何先验知识。经过训练的解码器在人工和网络管理的数据集以及标准图像分类基准的混合上进行了测试，获得了高达87.5%的细粒度无提示预测分数，考虑到该模型必须适用于任何可想象的图像并且没有任何上下文线索，这是一个强有力的结果。

[NLP-55] he Life Cycle of Large Language Models: A Review of Biases in Education
[NLP-55] 大型语言模型的生命周期：教育偏见评论

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11203
作者: Jinsook Lee,Yann Hicke,Renzhe Yu,Christopher Brooks,René F. Kizilcec
关键词: Large Language Models, Large Language, provide personalized support, LLM life cycle, students and teachers
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、大型语言、提供个性化支持、LLM生命周期、学生和教师
类目: Computers and Society (cs.CY); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 20 pages, 2 figures, preprint for British Journal of Educational Technology submission

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly adopted in educational contexts to provide personalized support to students and teachers. The unprecedented capacity of LLM-based applications to understand and generate natural language can potentially improve instructional effectiveness and learning outcomes, but the integration of LLMs in education technology has renewed concerns over algorithmic bias which may exacerbate educational inequities. In this review, building on prior work on mapping the traditional machine learning life cycle, we provide a holistic map of the LLM life cycle from the initial development of LLMs to customizing pre-trained models for various applications in educational settings. We explain each step in the LLM life cycle and identify potential sources of bias that may arise in the context of education. We discuss why current measures of bias from traditional machine learning fail to transfer to LLM-generated content in education, such as tutoring conversations because the text is high-dimensional, there can be multiple correct responses, and tailoring responses may be pedagogically desirable rather than unfair. This review aims to clarify the complex nature of bias in LLM applications and provide practical guidance for their evaluation to promote educational equity.
摘要：大型语言模型被越来越多地应用于教育领域，为学生和教师提供个性化的支持。基于LLM的应用程序理解和生成自然语言的能力前所未有，这可能会提高教学效率和学习结果，但LLMS在教育技术中的集成再次引发了人们对算法偏差的担忧，这可能会加剧教育不平等。在这篇综述中，在先前关于绘制传统机器学习生命周期的工作的基础上，我们提供了从LLMS的初始开发到为教育环境中的各种应用定制预训练模型的LLM生命周期的整体地图。我们解释了LLM生命周期中的每个步骤，并确定了在教育背景下可能出现的偏见的潜在来源。我们讨论了为什么目前的偏见措施，从传统的机器学习未能转移到LLM生成的教育内容，例如辅导对话，因为文本是高维的，可能有多个正确的反应，以及定制的反应可能是教学上希望的，而不是不公平的。本综述旨在澄清法律硕士申请中的偏见的复杂性，并为其评估提供实践指导，以促进教育公平。

[NLP-56] Actuation without production bias
[NLP-56] 无生产偏差的驱动

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11202
作者: James Kirby,Morgan Sonderegger
关键词: Phonetic production bias, production bias, Phonetic production, sound change, bias
中文关键词: 语音制作偏见，制作偏见，语音制作，声音变化，偏见
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Preprint of chapter to be published in Speech Dynamics: Synchronic Variation and Diachronic Change

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Phonetic production bias is the external force most commonly invoked in computational models of sound change, despite the fact that it is not responsible for all, or even most, sound changes. Furthermore, the existence of production bias alone cannot account for how changes do or do not propagate throughout a speech community. While many other factors have been invoked by (socio)phoneticians, including but not limited to contact (between subpopulations) and differences in social evaluation (of variants, groups, or individuals), these are not typically modeled in computational simulations of sound change. In this paper, we consider whether production biases have a unique dynamics in terms of how they impact the population-level spread of change in a setting where agents learn from multiple teachers. We show that, while the dynamics conditioned by production bias are not unique, it is not the case that all perturbing forces have the same dynamics: in particular, if social weight is a function of individual teachers and the correlation between a teacher’s social weight and the extent to which they realize a production bias is weak, change is unlikely to propagate. Nevertheless, it remains the case that changes initiated from different sources may display a similar dynamics. A more nuanced understanding of how population structure interacts with individual biases can thus provide a (partial) solution to the `non-phonologization problem’.
摘要：语音产生偏差是语音变化计算模型中最常用的外力，尽管它并不是所有甚至大多数语音变化的原因。此外，生产偏见的存在本身不能解释变化如何在整个演讲社区传播。虽然(社会)语音学家引用了许多其他因素，包括但不限于(亚群之间的)接触和(对变种、群体或个人的)社会评价的差异，这些因素通常不会在语音变化的计算模拟中建模。在本文中，我们考虑了在代理人向多名教师学习的情况下，生产偏差是否具有独特的动态，因为它们如何影响变化的人口水平传播。我们发现，虽然受生产偏差制约的动态并不是唯一的，但并不是所有干扰力量都具有相同的动态：特别是，如果社会权重是教师个人的函数，并且教师的社会权重与他们意识到生产偏差的程度之间的相关性很弱，那么变化不太可能传播。然而，从不同来源发起的变化可能表现出类似的动态，这一点仍然存在。因此，对群体结构如何与个体偏见相互作用的更细微的理解可以为“非音系化问题”提供(部分)解决方案。

[NLP-57] In Silico Sociology: Forecasting COVID-19 Polarization with Large Language Models
[NLP-57] Silico社会学：用大型语言模型预测COVID-19两极分化

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11190
作者: Austin C. Kozlowski,Hyunku Kwon,James A. Evans
关键词: large language models, training deep neural, deep neural networks, complex linguistic patterns, large language
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、训练深度神经、深度神经网络、复杂语言模式、大型语言
类目: Computers and Society (cs.CY); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:By training deep neural networks on massive archives of digitized text, large language models (LLMs) learn the complex linguistic patterns that constitute historic and contemporary discourses. We argue that LLMs can serve as a valuable tool for sociological inquiry by enabling accurate simulation of respondents from specific social and cultural contexts. Applying LLMs in this capacity, we reconstruct the public opinion landscape of 2019 to examine the extent to which the future polarization over COVID-19 was prefigured in existing political discourse. Using an LLM trained on texts published through 2019, we simulate the responses of American liberals and conservatives to a battery of pandemic-related questions. We find that the simulated respondents reproduce observed partisan differences in COVID-19 attitudes in 84% of cases, significantly greater than chance. Prompting the simulated respondents to justify their responses, we find that much of the observed partisan gap corresponds to differing appeals to freedom, safety, and institutional trust. Our findings suggest that the politicization of COVID-19 was largely consistent with the prior ideological landscape, and this unprecedented event served to advance history along its track rather than change the rails.
摘要：通过对海量数字化文本档案的深度神经网络进行训练，大型语言模型(LLM)学习构成历史和当代语篇的复杂语言模式。我们认为，通过能够准确模拟来自特定社会和文化背景的受访者，LLMS可以作为社会学调查的宝贵工具。应用最大似然模型，我们重构了2019年的舆论格局，以检验现有政治话语在多大程度上预示了未来围绕新冠肺炎的两极分化。使用对2019年出版的文本进行培训的LLM，我们模拟了美国自由派和保守派对一系列与流行病相关的问题的反应。我们发现，在84%的案例中，模拟受访者再现了观察到的新冠肺炎态度的党派差异，明显大于偶然性。促使模拟受访者为他们的反应辩护，我们发现，观察到的大部分党派分歧对应于对自由、安全和制度信任的不同诉求。我们的发现表明，新冠肺炎的政治化与之前的意识形态格局基本一致，这一史无前例的事件起到的是推动历史前进的作用，而不是改变轨道。

[NLP-58] FarsInstruct: Empowering Large Language Models for Persian Instruction Understanding
[NLP-58] FarsDirect：为波斯语指令理解提供支持的大型语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11186
作者: Hojjat Mokhtarabadi,Ziba Zamani,Abbas Maazallahi,Hossein Manshaei
关键词: Instruction-tuned large language, demonstrated remarkable capabilities, large language models, Instruction-tuned large, large language
中文关键词: 指令调整的大型语言，表现出非凡的能力，大型语言模型，指令调整的大型语言
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Instruction-tuned large language models, such as T0, have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in following instructions across various domains. However, their proficiency remains notably deficient in many low-resource languages. To address this challenge, we introduce FarsInstruct: a comprehensive instruction dataset designed to enhance the instruction-following ability of large language models specifically for the Persian language, a significant yet underrepresented language globally. FarsInstruct encompasses a wide range of task types and datasets, each containing a mix of straightforward to complex manual written instructions, as well as translations from Public Pool of Prompts, ensuring a rich linguistic and cultural representation. Furthermore, we introduce Co-CoLA, a framework designed to enhance the multi-task adaptability of LoRA-tuned models. Through extensive experimental analyses, our study showcases the effectiveness of FarsInstruct dataset coupled with training by Co-CoLA framework, in improving the performance of large language models within the Persian context. As of the current writing, FarsInstruct comprises more than 200 templates across 21 distinct datasets, and we intend to update it consistently, thus augmenting its applicability.
摘要：指令调谐的大型语言模型，如T0，在跟踪不同领域的指令方面表现出了非凡的能力。然而，他们对许多资源不足的语言的熟练程度仍然明显不足。为了应对这一挑战，我们引入了FarsInstruct：一个全面的教学数据集，旨在增强专门针对波斯语的大型语言模型的指令跟踪能力。波斯语是一种重要的但在全球范围内未得到充分代表的语言。FarsInstruct涵盖了广泛的任务类型和数据集，每个都包含简单到复杂的手动书面说明以及公共提示库的翻译，确保了丰富的语言和文化表现。此外，我们还引入了CoCola框架，该框架旨在增强Lora-Tuned模型的多任务适应性。通过大量的实验分析，我们的研究展示了FarsInstruct数据集与Co-Cola框架训练相结合的有效性，在波斯背景下提高了大型语言模型的性能。在撰写本文时，FarsInstruct包含21个不同数据集的200多个模板，我们打算一致地更新它，从而增强其适用性。

[NLP-59] YouTube-SL-25: A Large-Scale Open-Domain Multilingual Sign Language Parallel Corpus
[NLP-59] YouTube-SL-25：大规模开放领域多语言手语并行数据库

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11144
作者: Garrett Tanzer,Biao Zhang
关键词: machine learning research, American Sign Language, American Sign, sign languages, better-studied sign languages
中文关键词: 机器学习研究，美国手语，美国手语，手语，研究更好的手语
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Access YouTube-SL-25 at this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Even for better-studied sign languages like American Sign Language (ASL), data is the bottleneck for machine learning research. The situation is worse yet for the many other sign languages used by Deaf/Hard of Hearing communities around the world. In this paper, we present YouTube-SL-25, a large-scale, open-domain multilingual corpus of sign language videos with seemingly well-aligned captions drawn from YouTube. With 3000 hours of videos across 25 sign languages, YouTube-SL-25 is a) 3x the size of YouTube-ASL, b) the largest parallel sign language dataset to date, and c) the first or largest parallel dataset for many of its component languages. We provide baselines for sign-to-text tasks using a unified multilingual multitask model based on T5 and report scores on benchmarks across 4 sign languages. The results demonstrate that multilingual transfer benefits both higher- and lower-resource sign languages within YouTube-SL-25.
摘要：即使对于美国手语（ASC）等研究得更好的手语，数据也是机器学习研究的瓶颈。对于世界各地聋人/重听社区使用的许多其他手语来说，情况更糟。在本文中，我们介绍了YouTube-SL-25，这是一个大规模、开放域的多语言手语视频库，其标题似乎从YouTube中提取。YouTube-SL-25拥有涵盖25种手语的3000小时视频，a）是YouTube-ATL的3倍大小，b）迄今为止最大的并行手语数据集，c）其许多组成语言的第一个或最大的并行数据集。我们使用基于T5的统一多语言多任务模型为手语到文本任务提供基线，并报告4种手语的基准得分。结果表明，多语言传输对YouTube-SL-25中资源较多和资源较少的手语都有利。

[NLP-60] Building Intelligence Identification System via Large Language Model Watermarking: A Survey and Beyond
[NLP-60] 通过大型语言模型水印构建智能识别系统：调查及超越

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11100
作者: Xuhong Wang,Haoyu Jiang,Yi Yu,Jingru Yu,Yilun Lin,Ping Yi,Yingchun Wang,Qiao Yu,Li Li,Fei-Yue Wang
关键词: Large Large Language, Large Language Models, Large Large, Large Language, Language Models
中文关键词: 大型语言，大型语言模型，大型，大型语言，语言模型
类目: Cryptography and Security (cs.CR); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 59 pages, 7 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into diverse industries, posing substantial security risks due to unauthorized replication and misuse. To mitigate these concerns, robust identification mechanisms are widely acknowledged as an effective strategy. Identification systems for LLMs now rely heavily on watermarking technology to manage and protect intellectual property and ensure data security. However, previous studies have primarily concentrated on the basic principles of algorithms and lacked a comprehensive analysis of watermarking theory and practice from the perspective of intelligent identification. To bridge this gap, firstly, we explore how a robust identity recognition system can be effectively implemented and managed within LLMs by various participants using watermarking technology. Secondly, we propose a mathematical framework based on mutual information theory, which systematizes the identification process to achieve more precise and customized watermarking. Additionally, we present a comprehensive evaluation of performance metrics for LLM watermarking, reflecting participant preferences and advancing discussions on its identification applications. Lastly, we outline the existing challenges in current watermarking technologies and theoretical frameworks, and provide directional guidance to address these challenges. Our systematic classification and detailed exposition aim to enhance the comparison and evaluation of various methods, fostering further research and development toward a transparent, secure, and equitable LLM ecosystem.
摘要：大型语言模型(LLM)越来越多地集成到不同的行业中，由于未经授权的复制和误用，带来了巨大的安全风险。为了缓解这些担忧，强有力的身份识别机制被广泛认为是一种有效的战略。LLMS的识别系统现在严重依赖于水印技术来管理和保护知识产权，并确保数据安全。然而，以往的研究主要集中在算法的基本原理上，缺乏从智能识别的角度对水印的理论和实践进行全面的分析。为了弥补这一差距，首先，我们探讨了如何使用水印技术在LLMS中有效地实现和管理健壮的身份识别系统。其次，我们提出了一种基于互信息理论的数学框架，将识别过程系统化，以实现更精确和个性化的水印。此外，我们对LLM水印的性能指标进行了全面的评估，反映了参与者的偏好，并推动了对其识别应用的讨论。最后，我们概述了当前数字水印技术和理论框架中存在的挑战，并为应对这些挑战提供了方向性的指导。我们的系统分类和详细阐述旨在加强对各种方法的比较和评估，促进进一步的研究和开发，以建立一个透明、安全和公平的LLM生态系统。

[NLP-61] Show Dont Tell: Evaluating Large Language Models Beyond Textual Understanding with ChildPlay
[NLP-61] 节目不告诉：用ChildPlay评估超越文本理解的大型语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11068
作者: Gonçalo Hora de Carvalho,Robert Pollice,Oscar Knap
关键词: possess broader cognitive, broader cognitive functions, LEGO Connect Language, possess broader, cognitive functions
中文关键词: 拥有更广泛的认知，更广泛的认知功能，乐高连接语言，拥有更广泛的认知功能
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We explore the hypothesis that LLMs, such as GPT-3.5 and GPT-4, possess broader cognitive functions, particularly in non-linguistic domains. Our approach extends beyond standard linguistic benchmarks by incorporating games like Tic-Tac-Toe, Connect Four, and Battleship, encoded via ASCII, to assess strategic thinking and decision-making. To evaluate the models’ ability to generalize beyond their training data, we introduce two additional games. The first game, LEGO Connect Language (LCL), tests the models’ capacity to understand spatial logic and follow assembly instructions. The second game, the game of shapes, challenges the models to identify shapes represented by 1s within a matrix of zeros, further testing their spatial reasoning skills. This “show, don’t tell” strategy uses games instead of simply querying the models. Our results show that despite their proficiency on standard benchmarks, GPT-3.5 and GPT-4’s abilities to play and reason about fully observable games without pre-training is mediocre. Both models fail to anticipate losing moves in Tic-Tac-Toe and Connect Four, and they are unable to play Battleship correctly. While GPT-4 shows some success in the game of shapes, both models fail at the assembly tasks presented in the LCL game. These results suggest that while GPT models can emulate conversational proficiency and basic rule comprehension, their performance in strategic gameplay and spatial reasoning tasks is very limited. Importantly, this reveals a blind spot in current LLM benchmarks that we highlight with our gameplay benchmark suite ChildPlay (this https URL). Our findings provide a cautionary tale about claims of emergent intelligence and reasoning capabilities of LLMs that are roughly the size of GPT-3.5 and GPT-4.
摘要：我们探讨了一种假设，即GPT-3.5和GPT-4等LLM具有更广泛的认知功能，特别是在非语言领域。我们的方法超越了标准的语言基准，将Tic-Tac-Toe、Connect Four和战舰等以ASCII编码的游戏纳入其中，以评估战略思考和决策。为了评估模型超越其训练数据的泛化能力，我们引入了另外两个游戏。第一个游戏是乐高连接语言(LCL)，测试模型理解空间逻辑和遵循汇编指令的能力。第二个游戏是形状游戏，挑战模型在零的矩阵中识别由1表示的形状，进一步测试他们的空间推理技能。这种“展示，不要说”的策略使用了游戏，而不是简单地询问模型。结果表明，尽管S熟练掌握了标准基准，但他们在没有经过训练的情况下，对完全可观察的比赛进行比赛和推理的能力是一般的。这两款游戏都未能预测到Tic-Tac-Toe和Connect Four中的失败走法，而且它们也无法正确地玩战舰。虽然GPT-4在形状游戏中取得了一些成功，但这两种型号在LCL游戏中的组装任务中都失败了。这些结果表明，尽管GPT模型能够模拟会话熟练程度和基本规则理解，但它们在策略性游戏和空间推理任务中的表现非常有限。重要的是，这揭示了当前LLM基准中的一个盲点，我们在游戏基准套件儿童游戏(这个HTTPS URL)中突出了这一点。我们的发现提供了一个警示故事，说明LLMS的紧急智能和推理能力大致相当于GPT-3.5和GPT-4的大小。

[NLP-62] EfficientQAT: Efficient Quantization-Aware Training for Large Language Models
[NLP-62] EfficientQAT：针对大型语言模型的高效量化感知培训

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11062
作者: Mengzhao Chen,Wenqi Shao,Peng Xu,Jiahao Wang,Peng Gao,Kaipeng Zhang,Yu Qiao,Ping Luo
关键词: Large language models, Large language, modern natural language, natural language processing, artificial intelligence
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、大型语言、现代自然语言、自然语言处理、人工智能
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: An efficient and effective quantization technical to improve the performance of low-bits LMMs and LVLMs

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models (LLMs) are integral to modern natural language processing and artificial intelligence. However, they face challenges in managing their significant memory requirements. Although quantization-aware training (QAT) offers a solution by reducing memory consumption through low-bit representations with minimal accuracy loss, it demands substantial training resources to optimize model weights and quantization parameters. To address this, we propose Efficient Quantization-Aware Training (EfficientQAT), a novel quantization technique for compressing LLMs. EfficientQAT involves two consecutive phases: Block-wise training of all parameters (Block-AP) and end-to-end training of quantization parameters (E2E-QP). Block-AP sequentially conducts quantization-aware training for all parameters in each transformer block with block-wise reconstruction, maintaining efficiency by avoiding training the entire LLM. Initialized with quantized model, E2E-QP then trains only quantization parameters (step sizes) end-to-end, enhancing efficiency with a fixed quantized backbone and reduced trainable parameter count. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EfficientQAT outperforms previous quantization methods across a range of models, including base LLMs, instruction-tuned LLMs, and multimodal LLMs, with scales from 7B to 70B parameters at various quantization bits. For instance, EfficientQAT obtains a 2-bit Llama-2-70B model on a single A100-80GB GPU in 41 hours, with less than 3% accuracy degradation compared to the full precision (69.48 vs. 72.41). Notably, this INT2 quantized 70B model obtains a 1.67 accuracy gain over the Llama-2-13B model (69.48 vs. 67.81) while requiring less memory (19.2GB vs. 24.2GB). Code is available at this https URL.
摘要：大语言模型是现代自然语言处理和人工智能的重要组成部分。然而，它们在管理其巨大的内存需求方面面临挑战。虽然量化感知训练(QAT)提供了一种解决方案，通过以最小的精度损失通过低位表示来减少内存消耗，但它需要大量的训练资源来优化模型权重和量化参数。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一种新的量化压缩技术–高效量化感知训练(EfficientQAT)。EfficientQAT包括两个连续的阶段：所有参数的逐块训练(Block-AP)和量化参数的端到端训练(E2E-QP)。BLOCK-AP通过逐块重建对每个变换块中的所有参数进行量化感知训练，通过避免训练整个LLM来保持效率。E2E-QP使用量化模型进行初始化，然后端到端只训练量化参数(步长)，通过固定量化主干来提高效率，并减少可训练参数的数量。大量实验表明，EfficientQAT在一系列模型上的性能优于以往的量化方法，包括基本LLMS、指令调优LLM和多模式LLM，在不同的量化比特上，参数范围从7B到70B。例如，EfficientQAT在单个A100-80 GB GPU上在41小时内获得了2位Llama-2-70B模型，与全精度(69.48比72.41)相比，精度降幅不到3%。值得注意的是，这种INT2量化的70B模型比Llama-2-13B模型获得了1.67的精度提升(69.48比67.81)，同时需要更少的内存(19.2 GB比24.2 GB)。代码可在此HTTPS URL上找到。

[NLP-63] Was it Slander? Towards Exact Inversion of Generative Language Models
[NLP-63] 是诽谤吗？迈向生成语言模型的精确倒置

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11059
作者: Adrians Skapars,Edoardo Manino,Youcheng Sun,Lucas C. Cordeiro
关键词: Training large language, Training large, large language models, time and money, large language
中文关键词: 训练大型语言，训练大型语言模型，时间和金钱，大型语言
类目: Cryptography and Security (cs.CR); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 4 pages, 3 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Training large language models (LLMs) requires a substantial investment of time and money. To get a good return on investment, the developers spend considerable effort ensuring that the model never produces harmful and offensive outputs. However, bad-faith actors may still try to slander the reputation of an LLM by publicly reporting a forged output. In this paper, we show that defending against such slander attacks requires reconstructing the input of the forged output or proving that it does not exist. To do so, we propose and evaluate a search based approach for targeted adversarial attacks for LLMs. Our experiments show that we are rarely able to reconstruct the exact input of an arbitrary output, thus demonstrating that LLMs are still vulnerable to slander attacks.
摘要：训练大型语言模型（LLM）需要投入大量的时间和金钱。为了获得良好的投资回报，开发人员花费了相当多的精力确保模型永远不会产生有害和令人反感的输出。然而，恶意行为者仍然可能试图通过公开报告伪造的成果来诽谤法学硕士的声誉。在本文中，我们表明，防御此类诽谤攻击需要重建伪造输出的输入或证明它不存在。为此，我们提出并评估了一种基于搜索的方法，用于针对LLM的有针对性的对抗攻击。我们的实验表明，我们很少能够重建任意输出的确切输入，从而证明LLM仍然容易受到诽谤攻击。

[NLP-64] A Survey on LoRA of Large Language Models
[NLP-64] 大型语言模型LoRA调查

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11046
作者: Yuren Mao,Yuhang Ge,Yijiang Fan,Wenyi Xu,Yu Mi,Zhonghao Hu,Yunjun Gao
关键词: pluggable low-rank matrices, efficient fine-tuning paradigms, dense neural network, neural network layers, performed parameter efficient
中文关键词: 可插入低等级矩阵、高效的微调范式、密集神经网络、神经网络层、执行参数高效
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Low-Rank Adaptation~(LoRA), which updates the dense neural network layers with pluggable low-rank matrices, is one of the best performed parameter efficient fine-tuning paradigms. Furthermore, it has significant advantages in cross-task generalization and privacy-preserving. Hence, LoRA has gained much attention recently, and the number of related literature demonstrates exponential growth. It is necessary to conduct a comprehensive overview of the current progress on LoRA. This survey categorizes and reviews the progress from the perspectives of (1) downstream adaptation improving variants that improve LoRA’s performance on downstream tasks; (2) cross-task generalization methods that mix multiple LoRA plugins to achieve cross-task generalization; (3) efficiency-improving methods that boost the computation-efficiency of LoRA; (4) data privacy-preserving methods that use LoRA in federated learning; (5) application. Besides, this survey also discusses the future directions in this field.
摘要：低阶自适应(LORA)是性能最好的参数高效微调范例之一，它用可插补的低阶矩阵来更新密集的神经网络层。此外，它在跨任务泛化和隐私保护方面具有显著的优势。因此，洛拉最近受到了极大的关注，相关文献的数量呈指数级增长。有必要对LORA目前的进展情况进行全面概述。本文从以下几个方面对LORA的研究进展进行了分类和综述：(1)改进LORA在下游任务上性能的改进变量；(2)混合多个LORA插件以实现跨任务泛化的跨任务泛化方法；(3)提高LORA计算效率的效率改进方法；(4)将LORA用于联合学习的数据隐私保护方法；(5)应用。此外，本调查还讨论了该领域的未来发展方向。

[NLP-65] Hadamard Adapter: An Extreme Parameter-Efficient Adapter Tuning Method for Pre-trained Language Models
[NLP-65] Hadamard适配器：一种用于预训练语言模型的极其参数高效的适配器调优方法

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11033
作者: Yuyan Chen,Qiang Fu,Ge Fan,Lun Du,Jian-Guang Lou,Shi Han,Dongmei Zhang,Zhixu Li,Yanghua Xiao
关键词: Pre-trained Language models, Pre-trained Language, achieved great success, Recent years, Language models
中文关键词: 预训练语言模型，预训练语言，取得了巨大成功，近年来，语言模型
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Accepted to CIKM 2023 (Long Paper)

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent years, Pre-trained Language models (PLMs) have swept into various fields of artificial intelligence and achieved great success. However, most PLMs, such as T5 and GPT3, have a huge amount of parameters, fine-tuning them is often expensive and time consuming, and storing them takes up a lot of space. Therefore, it is necessary to adopt a parameter-efficient approach to reduce parameters of PLMs in fine-tuning without compromising their performance in downstream tasks. In this paper, we design a novel adapter which only acts on self-attention outputs in PLMs. This adapter adopts element-wise linear transformation using Hadamard product, hence named as Hadamard adapter, requires the fewest parameters compared to previous parameter-efficient adapters. In addition, we also summarize some tuning patterns for Hadamard adapter shared by various downstream tasks, expecting to provide some guidance for further parameter reduction with shared adapters in future studies. The experiments conducted on the widely-used GLUE benchmark with several SOTA PLMs prove that the Hadamard adapter achieves competitive performance with only 0.033% parameters compared with full fine-tuning, and it has the fewest parameters compared with other adapters. Moreover, we further find that there is also some redundant layers in the Hadamard adapter which can be removed to achieve more parameter efficiency with only 0.022% parameters.
摘要：近年来，预训练语言模型(PLM)席卷了人工智能的各个领域，并取得了巨大的成功。然而，大多数PLM，如T5和GPT3，具有大量的参数，微调它们往往是昂贵和耗时的，并且存储它们占用大量空间。因此，有必要在不影响下游任务性能的前提下，采用一种参数有效的方法来减少PLM在微调中的参数。在本文中，我们设计了一种新型的适配器，它只作用于PLM中的自我注意输出。该适配器采用基于Hadamard乘积的元素线性变换，因此称为Hadamard适配器，与以往的参数高效适配器相比，它需要的参数最少。此外，我们还总结了不同下游任务共享的Hadamard适配器的一些调优模式，以期为以后的研究中进一步使用共享适配器进行参数优化提供一些指导。在具有多个SOTA PLM的GLUE基准上进行的实验表明，与完全微调相比，Hadamard适配器仅需0.033个参数即可获得与之相当的性能，并且与其他适配器相比其参数最少。此外，我们还发现，Hadamard适配器中也存在一些冗余层，这些冗余层可以在仅0.022个参数的情况下消除，以获得更高的参数效率。

[NLP-66] DLO: Dynamic Layer Operation for Efficient Vertical Scaling of LLMs
[NLP-66] DLO：用于LLM高效垂直扩展的动态层操作

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11030
作者: Zhen Tan,Daize Dong,Xinyu Zhao,Jie Peng,Yu Cheng,Tianlong Chen
关键词: transformer-based Large Language, Dynamic Layer Operations, Large Language Models, scaling transformer-based Large, introduce Dynamic Layer
中文关键词: 基于转换器的大型语言、动态层操作、大型语言模型、扩展基于转换器的大型、引入动态层
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In this paper, we introduce Dynamic Layer Operations (DLO), a novel approach for vertically scaling transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs) by dynamically expanding, activating, or skipping layers using a sophisticated routing policy based on layerwise feature similarity. Unlike traditional Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) methods that focus on extending the model width, our approach targets model depth, addressing the redundancy observed across layer representations for various input samples. Our framework is integrated with the Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) stage, eliminating the need for resource-intensive Continual Pre-Training (CPT). Experimental results demonstrate that DLO not only outperforms the original unscaled models but also achieves comparable results to densely expanded models with significantly improved efficiency. Our work offers a promising direction for building efficient yet powerful LLMs. We will release our implementation and model weights upon acceptance.
摘要：在本文中，我们介绍了动态层操作（DLO），这是一种通过使用基于逐层特征相似性的复杂路由策略动态扩展、激活或跳过层来垂直扩展基于转换器的大型语言模型（LLM）的新颖方法。与专注于扩展模型宽度的传统专家混合（MoE）方法不同，我们的方法以模型深度为目标，解决在各个输入样本的层表示中观察到的冗余问题。我们的框架与监督微调（SFT）阶段集成，消除了对资源密集型连续预训练（CPD）的需求。实验结果表明，DLO不仅优于原始的未缩放模型，而且还获得了与密集扩展模型相当的结果，效率显着提高。我们的工作为构建高效而强大的LLM提供了一个有前途的方向。我们将在接受后发布我们的实现和模型权重。

[NLP-67] Efficacy of Various Large Language Models in Generating Smart Contracts
[NLP-67] 各种大型语言模型在生成智能合同方面的功效

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11019
作者: Siddhartha Chatterjee,Bina Ramamurthy
关键词: Large Language Models, code-generating Large Language, Evaluating Large Language, Language Models Trained, Ethereum Blockchain
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、代码生成大型语言、评估大型语言、语言模型训练、以太坊区块链
类目: oftware Engineering (cs.SE); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 10 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This study analyzes the application of code-generating Large Language Models in the creation of immutable Solidity smart contracts on the Ethereum Blockchain. Other works such as Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code, Mark Chen et. al (2012) have previously analyzed Artificial Intelligence code generation abilities. This paper aims to expand this to a larger scope to include programs where security and efficiency are of utmost priority such as smart contracts. The hypothesis leading into the study was that LLMs in general would have difficulty in rigorously implementing security details in the code, which was shown through our results, but surprisingly generally succeeded in many common types of contracts. We also discovered a novel way of generating smart contracts through new prompting strategies.
摘要：本研究分析了代码生成大型语言模型在以太坊区块链上创建不可变Solidity智能合约中的应用。其他作品，例如评估经过代码训练的大型语言模型，Mark Chen等人。al（2012）此前曾分析过人工智能代码生成能力。本文旨在将其扩展到更大的范围，以包括安全性和效率最重要的项目，例如智能合同。导致该研究的假设是，LLM总体上很难在代码中严格实现安全细节，这一点在我们的结果中得到了证明，但令人惊讶的是，LLM在许多常见类型的合同中普遍取得了成功。我们还发现了一种通过新的激励策略生成智能合同的新颖方法。

[NLP-68] Direct-Inverse Prompting: Analyzing LLMs Discriminative Capacity in Self-Improving Generation
[NLP-68] 正逆预算：分析LLM在自我改进一代中的辨别能力

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11017
作者: Jihyun Janice Ahn,Ryo Kamoi,Lu Cheng,Rui Zhang,Wenpeng Yin
关键词: Mainstream LLM research, Mainstream LLM, research has primarily, primarily focused, focused on enhancing
中文关键词: 主流法学硕士研究，主流法学硕士，研究主要集中在加强
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 4 pages, 3 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Mainstream LLM research has primarily focused on enhancing their generative capabilities. However, even the most advanced LLMs experience uncertainty in their outputs, often producing varied results on different runs or when faced with minor changes in input, despite no substantial change in content. Given multiple responses from the same LLM to the same input, we advocate leveraging the LLMs’ discriminative capability to reduce this generative uncertainty, aiding in identifying the correct answers. Specifically, we propose and analyze three discriminative prompts: direct, inverse, and hybrid, to explore the potential of both closed-source and open-source LLMs in self-improving their generative performance on two benchmark datasets. Our insights reveal which discriminative prompt is most promising and when to use it. To our knowledge, this is the first work to systematically analyze LLMs’ discriminative capacity to address generative uncertainty.
摘要：主流LLM研究主要集中在提高他们的生成能力。然而，即使是最先进的LLM的输出也会遇到不确定性，尽管内容没有重大变化，但经常在不同的运行中或在输入方面面临微小变化时产生不同的结果。鉴于同一LLM对同一输入的多个响应，我们主张利用LLM的辨别能力来减少这种生成的不确定性，帮助识别正确答案。具体来说，我们提出并分析了三种区分提示：直接、反向和混合，以探索闭源和开源LLM在两个基准数据集上自我改进生成性能的潜力。我们的见解揭示了哪种识别提示最有希望以及何时使用它。据我们所知，这是第一项系统性分析LLM识别能力以解决生成不确定性的工作。

[NLP-69] LongLaMP: A Benchmark for Personalized Long-form Text Generation
[NLP-69] LongLaMP：个性化长格式文本生成的基准

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11016
作者: Ishita Kumar,Snigdha Viswanathan,Sushrita Yerra,Alireza Salemi,Ryan A. Rossi,Franck Dernoncourt,Hanieh Deilamsalehy,Xiang Chen,Ruiyi Zhang,Shubham Agarwal,Nedim Lipka,Hamed Zamani
关键词: Long-text generation, personalized long-text generation, long-text generation tasks, Long-text, generation
中文关键词: 长文本生成，个性化长文本生成，长文本生成任务，长文本，生成
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 9 pages, 4 figures, 20 tables(including appendix) submitted to EMNLP

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Long-text generation is seemingly ubiquitous in real-world applications of large language models such as generating an email or writing a review. Despite the fundamental importance and prevalence of long-text generation in many practical applications, existing work on personalized generation has focused on the generation of very short text. To overcome these limitations, we study the problem of personalized long-text generation, that is, generating long-text that is personalized for a specific user while being practically useful for the vast majority of real-world applications that naturally require the generation of longer text. In this work, we demonstrate the importance of user-specific personalization for long-text generation tasks and develop the Long-text Language Model Personalization (LongLaMP) Benchmark. LongLaMP provides a comprehensive and diverse evaluation framework for personalized long-text generation. Extensive experiments on LongLaMP for zero-shot and fine-tuned language tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed benchmark and its utility for developing and evaluating techniques for personalized long-text generation across a wide variety of long-text generation tasks. The results highlight the importance of personalization across a wide variety of long-text generation tasks. Finally, we release the benchmark for others to use for this important problem.
摘要：在大型语言模型的真实应用中，长文本生成似乎无处不在，例如生成电子邮件或撰写评论。尽管长文本生成在许多实际应用中具有基本的重要性和普遍性，但现有的个性化生成工作主要集中在生成非常短的文本上。为了克服这些限制，我们研究了个性化长文本生成问题，即生成针对特定用户的个性化长文本，同时对自然需要生成更长文本的绝大多数现实世界应用程序具有实际用途。在这项工作中，我们论证了用户特定的个性化对于长文本生成任务的重要性，并开发了长文本语言模型个性化(LongLaMP)基准测试。LongLaMP为个性化的长文本生成提供了一个全面和多样化的评估框架。在LongLaMP上进行的零镜头和微调语言任务的大量实验证明了所提出的基准测试的有效性，以及它在开发和评估各种长文本生成任务中的个性化长文本生成技术方面的有效性。研究结果强调了个性化在各种长文本生成任务中的重要性。最后，我们发布基准，以供其他人使用来解决这个重要的问题。

[NLP-70] Does ChatGPT Have a Mind?
[NLP-70] ChatGPT有想法吗？

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11015
作者: Simon Goldstein,Benjamin A. Levinstein
关键词: Large Language Models, Language Models, Large Language, psychology encompassing beliefs, ChatGPT possess minds
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、语言模型、大型语言、包含信仰的心理学、ChatGPT拥有思想
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This paper examines the question of whether Large Language Models (LLMs) like ChatGPT possess minds, focusing specifically on whether they have a genuine folk psychology encompassing beliefs, desires, and intentions. We approach this question by investigating two key aspects: internal representations and dispositions to act. First, we survey various philosophical theories of representation, including informational, causal, structural, and teleosemantic accounts, arguing that LLMs satisfy key conditions proposed by each. We draw on recent interpretability research in machine learning to support these claims. Second, we explore whether LLMs exhibit robust dispositions to perform actions, a necessary component of folk psychology. We consider two prominent philosophical traditions, interpretationism and representationalism, to assess LLM action dispositions. While we find evidence suggesting LLMs may satisfy some criteria for having a mind, particularly in game-theoretic environments, we conclude that the data remains inconclusive. Additionally, we reply to several skeptical challenges to LLM folk psychology, including issues of sensory grounding, the “stochastic parrots” argument, and concerns about memorization. Our paper has three main upshots. First, LLMs do have robust internal representations. Second, there is an open question to answer about whether LLMs have robust action dispositions. Third, existing skeptical challenges to LLM representation do not survive philosophical scrutiny.
摘要：本文考察了像ChatGPT这样的大型语言模型(LLM)是否拥有头脑的问题，特别关注他们是否具有真正的民间心理，包括信仰、欲望和意图。我们通过调查两个关键方面来处理这个问题：内部陈述和行动的倾向。首先，我们回顾了各种关于表征的哲学理论，包括信息论、因果论、结构论和目的论，认为LLM满足各自提出的关键条件。我们利用最近机器学习中的可解释性研究来支持这些说法。其次，我们探索LLM是否表现出强健的性情来执行动作，这是民间心理学的必要组成部分。我们考虑了两个重要的哲学传统，即解释主义和表征主义，来评估LLM的行动倾向。虽然我们发现证据表明，LLM可能满足拥有头脑的一些标准，特别是在博弈论环境中，但我们得出的结论是，这些数据仍然没有定论。此外，我们回答了几个对LLM民间心理学持怀疑态度的挑战，包括感官基础的问题，“随机鹦鹉”的论点，以及对记忆的担忧。我们的论文有三个主要结论。首先，LLM确实拥有强大的内部表示。其次，关于LLM是否具有强大的行动倾向，还有一个悬而未决的问题需要回答。第三，对法学院代表权的现有怀疑挑战经不起哲学的审视。

[NLP-71] Geode: A Zero-shot Geospatial Question-Answering Agent with Explicit Reasoning and Precise Spatio-Temporal Retrieval
[NLP-71] Geode：具有显式推理和精确时空检索的零镜头地理空间搜索代理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11014
作者: Devashish Vikas Gupta,Azeez Syed Ali Ishaqui,Divya Kiran Kadiyala
关键词: Large language models, shown promising results, Large language, Natural Language Processing, shown promising
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，显示出有希望的结果，大型语言、自然语言处理，显示出有希望
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Multiagent Systems (cs.MA)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models (LLMs) have shown promising results in learning and contextualizing information from different forms of data. Recent advancements in foundational models, particularly those employing self-attention mechanisms, have significantly enhanced our ability to comprehend the semantics of diverse data types. One such area that could highly benefit from multi-modality is in understanding geospatial data, which inherently has multiple modalities. However, current Natural Language Processing (NLP) mechanisms struggle to effectively address geospatial queries. Existing pre-trained LLMs are inadequately equipped to meet the unique demands of geospatial data, lacking the ability to retrieve precise spatio-temporal data in real-time, thus leading to significantly reduced accuracy in answering complex geospatial queries. To address these limitations, we introduce Geode–a pioneering system designed to tackle zero-shot geospatial question-answering tasks with high precision using spatio-temporal data retrieval. Our approach represents a significant improvement in addressing the limitations of current LLM models, demonstrating remarkable improvement in geospatial question-answering abilities compared to existing state-of-the-art pre-trained models.
摘要：大型语言模型(LLM)在从不同形式的数据中学习信息和将信息语境化方面取得了很好的结果。基础模型的最新进展，特别是那些使用自我注意机制的模型，显著增强了我们理解不同数据类型的语义的能力。可以从多模式中获益的一个这样的领域是理解地理空间数据，而地理空间数据本身就具有多种模式。然而，当前的自然语言处理(NLP)机制难以有效地解决地理空间查询。现有的预先训练的土地管理系统装备不足，无法满足地理空间数据的独特需求，缺乏实时检索精确时空数据的能力，从而导致回答复杂地理空间查询的准确性大大降低。为了解决这些限制，我们引入了Geode–一个开创性的系统，旨在使用时空数据检索来高精度地处理零镜头地理空间问答任务。我们的方法代表着在解决当前LLM模型的局限性方面的重大改进，表明与现有最先进的预先训练的模型相比，地理空间问答能力有了显著的改进。

[NLP-72] Exploring Gender-Specific Speech Patterns in Automatic Suicide Risk Assessment
[NLP-72] 在自动自杀风险评估中探索特定性别的言语模式

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11012
作者: Maurice Gerczuk,Shahin Amiriparian,Justina Lutz,Wolfgang Strube,Irina Papazova,Alkomiet Hasan,Björn W. Schuller
关键词: specialised psychiatric care, emergency medicine, timely intervention, psychiatric care, hindered by delayed
中文关键词: 专门的精神科护理、急诊医学、及时干预、精神科护理、因延误而受阻
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: accepted at INTERSPEECH 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In emergency medicine, timely intervention for patients at risk of suicide is often hindered by delayed access to specialised psychiatric care. To bridge this gap, we introduce a speech-based approach for automatic suicide risk assessment. Our study involves a novel dataset comprising speech recordings of 20 patients who read neutral texts. We extract four speech representations encompassing interpretable and deep features. Further, we explore the impact of gender-based modelling and phrase-level normalisation. By applying gender-exclusive modelling, features extracted from an emotion fine-tuned wav2vec2.0 model can be utilised to discriminate high- from low- suicide risk with a balanced accuracy of 81%. Finally, our analysis reveals a discrepancy in the relationship of speech characteristics and suicide risk between female and male subjects. For men in our dataset, suicide risk increases together with agitation while voice characteristics of female subjects point the other way.
摘要：在急诊医学中，对有自杀风险的患者的及时干预经常因延迟获得专门的精神护理而受到阻碍。为了弥补这一差距，我们引入了一种基于语音的方法来自动评估自杀风险。我们的研究涉及一个新的数据集，包括20名阅读中性文本的患者的语音记录。我们提取了四个包含可解释特征和深层特征的语音表示。此外，我们还探讨了基于性别的建模和短语级别标准化的影响。通过应用性别专属建模，从情绪微调的Wave2ve2.0模型中提取的特征可以用来区分高自杀风险和低自杀风险，均衡的准确率为81%。最后，我们的分析显示，在言语特征与自杀风险的关系上，女性和男性之间存在差异。在我们的数据集中，男性的自杀风险随着焦虑的增加而增加，而女性受试者的声音特征则指向相反的方向。

[NLP-73] Navigating the Minefield of MT Beam Search in Cascaded Streaming Speech Translation
[NLP-73] 级联流语音翻译中MT束搜索雷区的导航

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11010
作者: Rastislav Rabatin,Frank Seide,Ernie Chang
关键词: speech translation system, cascaded real-time speech, well-known beam-search algorithm, real-time speech translation, adapt the well-known
中文关键词: 语音翻译系统，级联实时语音，知名的射束搜索算法，实时语音翻译，适应知名的
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We adapt the well-known beam-search algorithm for machine translation to operate in a cascaded real-time speech translation system. This proved to be more complex than initially anticipated, due to four key challenges: (1) real-time processing of intermediate and final transcriptions with incomplete words from ASR, (2) emitting intermediate and final translations with minimal user perceived latency, (3) handling beam search hypotheses that have unequal length and different model state, and (4) handling sentence boundaries. Previous work in the field of simultaneous machine translation only implemented greedy decoding. We present a beam-search realization that handles all of the above, providing guidance through the minefield of challenges. Our approach increases the BLEU score by 1 point compared to greedy search, reduces the CPU time by up to 40% and character flicker rate by 20+% compared to a baseline heuristic that just retranslates input repeatedly.
摘要：我们采用了著名的机器翻译束搜索算法，使其在级联实时语音翻译系统中运行。事实证明，由于四个关键挑战，这比最初预期的更加复杂：（1）实时处理带有来自ASB的不完整单词的中间和最终翻译，（2）以最小的用户感知延迟发出中间和最终翻译，（3）处理具有不等长度和不同模型状态的束搜索假设，以及（4）处理句子边界。机器同步翻译领域的先前工作仅实现了贪婪解码。我们提出了一种可以处理上述所有问题的射束搜索实现，为挑战雷区提供指导。与贪婪搜索相比，我们的方法将BLEU分数提高了1个百分点，与只是重复重新翻译输入的基线启发式相比，将处理器时间减少了40%，将字符闪烁率减少了20+%。

[NLP-74] CharED: Character-wise Ensemble Decoding for Large Language Models
[NLP-74] CharED：大型语言模型的逐行集成解码

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11009
作者: Kevin Gu,Eva Tuecke,Dmitriy Katz,Raya Horesh,David Alvarez-Melis,Mikhail Yurochkin
关键词: Large language models, shown remarkable potential, achieving increasingly impressive, Large language, increasingly impressive performance
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，展现出非凡的潜力，取得越来越令人印象深刻的成就，大型语言，越来越令人印象深刻的性能
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 9 pages, 4 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable potential for problem solving, with open source models achieving increasingly impressive performance on benchmarks measuring areas from logical reasoning to mathematical ability. Ensembling models can further improve capabilities across a variety of domains. However, conventional methods of combining models at inference time such as shallow fusion necessitate a shared vocabulary and tokenization, and alternatives like fine-tuning for domain-specific performance are both time consuming and computationally expensive. We therefore present an inference-time ensembling algorithm aimed at “averaging” outputs from multiple LLMs and illustrate its improved performance across multiple domains compared to its constituent models alone. Character-wise ensemble decoding, CharED, finds the marginal distribution of each character for an individual model and performs a weighted average to generate an output, character by character. In coding, math, and toxicity benchmarks, we find our proposed model able to combine complimentary strengths of multiple LLMs, regardless of vocabulary, tokenization, or model size.
摘要：大型语言模型在解决问题方面显示出了巨大的潜力，开源模型在从逻辑推理到数学能力的基准测试方面取得了越来越令人印象深刻的表现。整合模型可以进一步提高各种领域的能力。然而，在推理时组合模型的传统方法(如浅层融合)需要共享词汇表和标记化，而针对特定领域的性能进行微调等替代方法既耗时又计算昂贵。因此，我们提出了一种推理时间集成算法，旨在对多个LLM的输出进行平均，并说明了与其组成模型相比，该算法在多个域上的性能改善。基于字符的集成解码，CHARED，找到单个模型中每个字符的边缘分布，并执行加权平均以生成逐个字符的输出。在编码、数学和毒性基准测试中，我们发现我们提出的模型能够结合多个LLM的互补优势，而不考虑词汇、标记化或模型大小。

[NLP-75] Figuring out Figures: Using Textual References to Caption Scientific Figures
[NLP-75] 弄清楚数字：使用文本参考来描述科学数字

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11008
作者: Stanley Cao,Kevin Liu
关键词: densely communicating complex, communicating complex ideas, essential channels, channels for densely, densely communicating
中文关键词: 密集沟通的综合体，沟通复杂的想法，必要的渠道，密集沟通的渠道
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Figures are essential channels for densely communicating complex ideas in scientific papers. Previous work in automatically generating figure captions has been largely unsuccessful and has defaulted to using single-layer LSTMs, which no longer achieve state-of-the-art performance. In our work, we use the SciCap datasets curated by Hsu et al. and use a variant of a CLIP+GPT-2 encoder-decoder model with cross-attention to generate captions conditioned on the image. Furthermore, we augment our training pipeline by creating a new dataset MetaSciCap that incorporates textual metadata from the original paper relevant to the figure, such as the title, abstract, and in-text references. We use SciBERT to encode the textual metadata and use this encoding alongside the figure embedding. In our experimentation with different models, we found that the CLIP+GPT-2 model performs better when it receives all textual metadata from the SciBERT encoder in addition to the figure, but employing a SciBERT+GPT2 model that uses only the textual metadata achieved optimal performance.
摘要：图形是科技论文中密集传达复杂思想的重要渠道。以前在自动生成图形字幕方面的工作在很大程度上是不成功的，默认使用单层LSTM，它不再实现最先进的性能。在我们的工作中，我们使用了由Hsu等人管理的SciCap数据集。并使用具有交叉注意的CLIP+GPT-2编解码器模型的变体来生成以图像为条件的字幕。此外，我们通过创建一个新的数据集MetaSciCap来增强我们的培训渠道，该数据集纳入了与图形相关的原始论文的文本元数据，如标题、摘要和文本中的引用。我们使用SciBERT对文本元数据进行编码，并将此编码与图形嵌入一起使用。在我们用不同模型进行的实验中，我们发现，当CLIP+GPT-2模型接收到除图形之外的所有文本元数据时，它的性能更好，但使用仅使用文本元数据的SciBERT+GPT2模型获得了最佳性能。

[NLP-76] Panacea: A foundation model for clinical trial search summarization design and recruitment
[NLP-76] 万能药：临床试验搜索摘要设计和招募的基础模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11007
作者: Jiacheng Lin,Hanwen Xu,Zifeng Wang,Sheng Wang,Jimeng Sun
关键词: clinical trial, trial, clinical trial tasks, Clinical, medical devices
中文关键词: 临床试验、试验、临床试验任务、临床、医疗设备
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Clinical trials are fundamental in developing new drugs, medical devices, and treatments. However, they are often time-consuming and have low success rates. Although there have been initial attempts to create large language models (LLMs) for clinical trial design and patient-trial matching, these models remain task-specific and not adaptable to diverse clinical trial tasks. To address this challenge, we propose a clinical trial foundation model named Panacea, designed to handle multiple tasks, including trial search, trial summarization, trial design, and patient-trial matching. We also assemble a large-scale dataset, named TrialAlign, of 793,279 trial documents and 1,113,207 trial-related scientific papers, to infuse clinical knowledge into the model by pre-training. We further curate TrialInstruct, which has 200,866 of instruction data for fine-tuning.
